Inside Deadly 60. Wildlife series. Steve takes a look inside some of the top predators featured on Deadly 60 to find out what makes them so deadly.
Endangered Special. Steve Backshall looks at animals that are both deadly and endangered, including the chimpanzee, mountain gorilla and harpy eagle, as well as the bizarre aye-aye.
8/8. It's crunch time for Waterloo Road - will it stay open? Chlo and Donte's futures are on the line as their case comes to court, and Lorna is devastated by Tom and Izzie's betrayal.
9/12. It's week 9, and the candidates take on TV selling, selecting products which they hope will win over the nation before selling their socks off on a TV shopping channel.
Rapper Pa Salieu returns to the stage after prison, turning a second chance into music. Contains very strong language and some upsetting scenes.
5/6. The boarders are stressed before final exams. With so many distractions, can they succeed? Contains strong language.
6/6. Leah is pushed to breaking point as the boarders prepare to say goodbye to St Gilbert's. Contains some strong language.
1/6. Conor and Jock get roped into an amateur heist masterminded by Billy Murphy, but drafting in baby Star causes chaos. Contains strong language.
2/6. Sgt Healy finally corners the lads, so they enlist the help of exasperated Mairead. Contains strong language.
3/6. Mairead books the lads a behaviour therapy session in Dublin, so a road trip is arranged. Contains strong language.
4/6. Conor and Jock are hoping to make the school debs a special night for the girls, but they're destined to create chaos despite themselves. Contains very strong language.
5/6. Jock gets caught up in a row with a local tearaway, leading to a challenge to a boxing match, which Jock reluctantly agrees to. Contains very strong language.
6/6. Conor and Jock decide it's time to move out of home to Spain, to Mairead's amusement. But an incident with Billy Murphy distracts them from their plan. Contains strong language.
